mission.title: Install and Configure youki
mission.description: youki is a CNCF container runtime implementation in Rust, providing a low-memory-footprint alternative to runc. It implements the OCI Runtime Spec and can be used as a drop-in replacement for runc with Docker or containerd.

steps.title: Download youki binary
steps.description:
Download the pre-built youki binary for Linux.

```bash
curl -sSL -o youki https://github.com/youki-dev/youki/releases/download/v0.6.0/youki_v0_6_0_linux_amd64
chmod +x youki
sudo mv youki /usr/local/bin/
```

> Requires Linux with kernel 5.3+.

steps.title: Configure Docker to use youki
steps.description:
Register youki as a Docker runtime.

```bash
sudo dockerd --experimental --add-runtime="youki=/usr/local/bin/youki" &
docker run --runtime=youki hello-world
```

> youki can also be configured in /etc/docker/daemon.json.
